what: mktemp template needs enough X’s, and a failed mktemp leaves an empty var
title: drift
text: BSD/macOS mktemp -d -t <name> requires template contain least 3 Xs (XXXXXX safe default). Without , mktemp errors stderr (X's template) prints nothing stdout. Pattern: SCRATCH=$(mktemp -d -t aider-smoke) && cd "$SCRATCH" && <destructive>. mktemp fails, $SCRATCH="". cd "" -op leaves caller’s cwd. destructive command (rm, git init, git add+commit) runs cwd instead throwaway tmpdir. Caught hard way 2026-05-13: Claude smoke test inside rtj checkout exactly , accidentally committed demo.R active feature branch, rode squash-merge rtj/main cleaned post-merge. Always use XXXXXX (6 X’s) template: mktemp -d -t aider-smoke.XXXXXX. Guard result: SCRATCH=$(mktemp -d ...) || exit 1; [ -n "$SCRATCH" ] || exit 1. Use set -euo pipefail failed command-substitution kills script.

what: BSD vs GNU sed/grep portability (macOS hits this constantly)
title: drift
text: macOS ships BSD sed/grep. Linux CI/cloud-init hosts ship GNU. Snippets work one silently misbehave . Symptom seen 2026-05-28: sed 's/[^-z0-9]\+/-/g' macOS left spaces issue-title slug, producing invalid git branch name. Fix: use sed -E (POSIX ERE) +, |, ?, (...) work without escapes flavors. regex becomes sed -E 's/[^-z0-9]+/-/g'. s|pat|repl| delimiter conflicts | alternation/replacement BSD. Pick delimiter appear pattern replacement (#, ,, : common choices). Compound s|x|y|; s|^| /|| chains trailing || looks like empty delimiter break BSD sed even GNU accepts . Use find <dir> -maxdepth 1 -mindepth 1 -type d -exec basename {} \; directory listings, printf '%s\n' <dir>/*/ glob, d <dir>/*/; basename "$d"; done. writing snippet expect ship skills/ SKILL.md cloud-init runcmd: must POSIX-portable. Default sed -E, avoid \+/\|, don’t pipe ls.

what: “Has been deleted” in plan output is not authoritative — verify against the cloud API first
title: drift
text: AWS provider (5.x 6.x) known class bug transient read error (false 404, regional-endpoint hiccup) interpreted “resource deleted outside OpenTofu.” plan show resource children scheduled destroy + recreate (forces replacement cascades children interpolate parent’s id/arn). didn’t delete resource plan says ’s gone, verify cloud API applying: aws s3 head-bucket --bucket X, aws iam get-role --role-name X, etc. tofu plan -refresh=true re-run moment later often reports “changes.” Caught 2026-05-14 rtj env/prod stac-era5-land: bucket fully intact (60 objects, 307 MB) plan said deleted 5 child resources “must replaced.” Apply clobbered policy + lifecycle configs still-existing bucket. Recovery via -target unrelated resource added (rtj#157 codifies lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true } bucket + load-bearing children). Belt--suspenders defense: add lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true } high-value resources (S3 buckets, RDS instances, anything irreplaceable) module. Tofu refuse plan destroy lifecycle line removed config — converts failure mode “apply silently clobbers” “plan errors Instance destroyed.” Don’t apply count-based resources count: 1 → 0 legitimate transition.

what: pkill <R/Python/etc. client> does NOT cancel its Postgres query
title: drift
text: Killing client (R, Python, psql) closes connection. libpq backend server keeps running -flight query finishes — server-side orphan. orphaned backend holds whatever locks (table, view, advisory). Every later DROP VIEW / LOCK TABLE / ALTER object blocks behind indefinitely — silent hangs indistinguishable slow query. Caught 2026-05-25 link#205: pkill’d wsg_run_one.R left frs_network_features SELECT running 1h45m; subsequent recomputes wedged DROP VIEW barriers_bt_access 1h08m someone noticed. Always terminate server-side backend, just client: kill client. Order matters don’t know side block.
code: SELECT pid, pg_terminate_backend(pid) FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE datname='<db>' AND state='active' AND now()-query_start > interval '3 minutes'   AND pid <> pg_backend_pid();

what: Set statement_timeout + lock_timeout on long DB ops
title: drift
text: long-running DB op R/Python/etc. client set session start, ideally via env (PGOPTIONS='-c statement_timeout=600000 -c lock_timeout=60000') connection (DBI::dbExecute(conn, "SET statement_timeout = '600000'")). runaway query cancels server-side (orphan); blocked DROP VIEW gives rather wedging behind zombie lock. Without , silent hangs become indistinguishable “still working” wait hours. Pick generous--bounded timeout (10× expected query time). point isn’t tight enforcement — ’s “fail loud instead fail silent.”

what: View vs. real table changes the planner’s join direction
title: drift
text: CREATE VIEW v SELECT * big_table … carries row-count statistics. Used join input, planner may pick side (big) outer driver, blowing nested-loop cost ~1000× — symptom looks like “indexes aren’t used” ’s actually wrong-direction nested loop. Caught link#205: AOI-scoping streams via VIEW left Postgres thinking 26k FINA segments big 800k persist barriers; picked barriers outer; 71M estimated result rows; >10 min wall. Fix AOI-scoping smaller dataset: materialise real CREATE TABLE indexes + ANALYZE. planner sees small row count picks outer. Drop table .exit ’s transient.

Tree loss agriculture (Crops + Rangeland + Bare Ground) 2017–2023. Percent pixels classified Trees 2017.
code: ag_classes <- c("Crops", "Rangeland", "Bare Ground")  # All transitions from Trees to get total Trees-origin pixel count all_from_trees <- dft_rast_transition(classified, from = "2017", to = "2023",                                        from_class = "Trees") total_tree_cells <- sum(all_from_trees$summary$n_cells)  # Filter to agriculture classes tree_loss <- dft_rast_transition(classified, from = "2017", to = "2023",                                   from_class = "Trees",                                   to_class = ag_classes)  # Relabel as Agriculture and compute pct of all Trees-origin pixels tree_loss_tbl <- tree_loss$summary |>   dplyr::mutate(to_class = "Agriculture") |>   dplyr::group_by(from_class, to_class) |>   dplyr::summarize(n_cells = sum(n_cells), area = sum(area), .groups = "drop") |>   dplyr::mutate(pct_of_trees = round(n_cells / total_tree_cells * 100, 2))  knitr::kable(tree_loss_tbl, digits = 2,              caption = "Tree loss to agriculture (Crops + Rangeland + Bare Ground) 2017--2023. code: patch_min <- 5000 n_pixels <- patch_min / prod(terra::res(classified[[1]]))  result_filtered <- dft_rast_transition(classified, from = "2017", to = "2023",                                        patch_area_min = patch_min)  changed_filtered <- result_filtered$summary |>   dplyr::filter(from_class != to_class) |>   dplyr::filter(pct >= 1) |>   dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(area))  # Comparison table: unfiltered vs filtered comparison <- changed |>   dplyr::select(from_class, to_class, n_cells, area) |>   dplyr::left_join(     changed_filtered |>       dplyr::select(from_class, to_class,                     n_cells_filtered = n_cells, area_filtered = area),     by = c("from_class", "to_class")   ) |>   dplyr::mutate(     dplyr::across(c(n_cells_filtered, area_filtered), ~tidyr::replace_na(.x, 0)),     cells_removed = n_cells - n_cells_filtered,     area_removed = area - area_filtered   ) knitr::kable(comparison, digits = 2, col.names = c(   "From", "To", "Cells", "Area (ha)", "Cells (filtered)",   "Area (filtered)", "Cells removed", "Area removed" ), caption = paste0(   "Land cover transitions 2017--2023: unfiltered vs filtered (min patch area ",   format(patch_min, big.mark = ","), " m\u00b2).")) aoi_proj <- sf::st_geometry(sf::st_transform(aoi, terra::crs(r_change)))  par(mfrow = c(1, 3)) terra::plot(r_change, main = "Unfiltered", axes = FALSE, mar = c(1, 1, 2, 6)) plot(aoi_proj, add = TRUE, border = "red", lwd = 2) terra::plot(result_filtered$removed, main = "Removed patches", axes = FALSE,             mar = c(1, 1, 2, 6)) plot(aoi_proj, add = TRUE, border = "red", lwd = 2) terra::plot(result_filtered$raster, main = paste0("Filtered (min ",             format(patch_min, big.mark = ","), " m\u00b2)"),             axes = FALSE, mar = c(1, 1, 2, 6)) plot(aoi_proj, add = TRUE, border = "red", lwd = 2)

title: Land Cover Change Detection for Floodplains

code: patches <- dft_transition_vectors(result$raster)  # Only actual changes (exclude same-class "transitions") patches_changed <- patches[grepl("->", patches$transition) &   !sapply(strsplit(patches$transition, " -> "), \(x) x[1] == x[2]), ]  knitr::kable(   head(sf::st_drop_geometry(patches_changed[order(-patches_changed$area_ha), ]), 10),   digits = 2,   caption = "Ten largest change patches (same-class transitions excluded)." ) aoi$zone <- "Neexdzii Kwa floodplain" patches_zoned <- dft_transition_vectors(result$raster, zones = aoi,                                         zone_col = "zone") cat("Patches inside AOI:", nrow(patches_zoned), "of", nrow(patches), "total\n") #> Patches inside AOI: 165 of 165 total

title: Trajectories as a Check on Land-Cover Change
text: dft_rast_break() measures size abrupt drop kNDVI. strong losses coherent blocks — shape harvest — scattered noise. beige outlines stands land-cover product maps Trees Rangeland. Break magnitude (red = large kNDVI drop). Beige outlines LULC Trees--Rangeland. red blocks sit inside outlines (confirmed, dated harvest); fall outside (removal categorical map missed); outlines carry break (label change without real canopy loss). three cases figure whole point. Red inside outline harvest methods agree — break dates . Red outside outlines real canopy loss categorical product flag. outline red mapped transition backed spectral drop — likely borderline pixel changing label rather trees coming . two products catch different real things; used together complete either alone.

title: Trajectories as a Check on Land-Cover Change

Much open floodplain greening; decline concentrates harvested blocks channel edges.

code: library(leaflet)  aoi4326  <- sf::st_transform(aoi, 4326) bb       <- sf::st_bbox(aoi4326) break_ll <- terra::project(break_mag, "EPSG:4326") trend_ll <- terra::project(trend[["trend"]], "EPSG:4326") pal_bk   <- colorNumeric(hcl.colors(11, "Blue-Red 3", rev = TRUE),                          domain = c(-0.45, 0.45), na.color = "transparent") pal_tr   <- colorNumeric(hcl.colors(11, "Blue-Red 3", rev = TRUE),                          domain = c(-0.04, 0.04), na.color = "transparent") basemaps <- c("Light" = "CartoDB.Positron",               "Esri Satellite" = "Esri.WorldImagery",               "Google Satellite" = "https://mt1.google.com/vt/lyrs=s&x={x}&y={y}&z={z}",               "OpenTopoMap" = "OpenTopoMap")  m <- leaflet::leaflet() |>   leaflet::setView(mean(bb[c("xmin", "xmax")]), mean(bb[c("ymin", "ymax")]),                    zoom = 14) for (i in seq_along(basemaps)) {   bm <- basemaps[[i]]   nm <- names(basemaps)[i]   m <- if (grepl("^https?://", bm)) {     leaflet::addTiles(m, urlTemplate = bm, group = nm)   } else {     leaflet::addProviderTiles(m, bm, group = nm)   } } overlays <- c("Break magnitude", "Trend (per yr)", "LULC Trees -> Rangeland", "AOI") m <- m |>   leaflet::addRasterImage(break_ll, colors = pal_bk, opacity = 0.8,                           group = "Break magnitude", project = FALSE) |>   leaflet::addRasterImage(trend_ll, colors = pal_tr, opacity = 0.8,                           group = "Trend (per yr)", project = FALSE) |>   leaflet::addPolygons(data = sf::st_transform(tree_trans, 4326),                        fillColor = "#e3e2c3", fillOpacity = 0.6,                        color = "#b3b083", weight = 1,                        group = "LULC Trees -> Rangeland") |>   leaflet::addPolygons(data = aoi4326, fill = FALSE, color = "red", weight = 2,                        group = "AOI") |>   leaflet::addLayersControl(     baseGroups = names(basemaps), overlayGroups = overlays,     options = leaflet::layersControlOptions(collapsed = FALSE)   ) |>   leaflet::hideGroup("Trend (per yr)") #> Warning in colors(.): Some values were outside the color scale and will be #> treated as NA if (requireNamespace("leaflet.extras", quietly = TRUE)) {   m <- leaflet.extras::addFullscreenControl(m) } m

title: Detect per-pixel index-trajectory breakpoints — dft_rast_break
text: cube monthly index SpatRaster (return value dft_stac_cube()): one layer per time step, time value per layer. history Character. bfastmonitor history-selection method: "" (default), "ROC", "BP". start Numeric c(year, period). Start monitoring period, stack's temporal frequency (e.g. c(2022, 1) = Jan 2022 monthly stack). Everything stable history. frequency Numeric NULL. Seasonal frequency time series (12 monthly, 1 annual). NULL, derived layer time spacing; supplied, must agree spacing call errors. order Integer. Harmonic order season-trend model passed bfast::bfastmonitor() (default 3). Lower (1-2) series samples part year (e.g. growing-season-cube dft_stac_cube() months), high order overfits sparse seasonal coverage. level Numeric. Significance level passed bfast::bfastmonitor() (default 0.01). min_obs Integer. Minimum non-NA observations required attempt fit; pixels fewer return NA (default 6). cores Integer NULL. Forked workers per-pixel reduction. NULL, uses one fewer detected cores.

title: Detect per-pixel index-trajectory breakpoints — dft_rast_break
text: two-band terra::SpatRaster layers break_date (decimal year NA) break_mag (signed index change; negative = index drop).

title: Detect per-pixel index-trajectory breakpoints — dft_rast_break
text: bfastmonitor fits season-trend model stable history period, watches monitoring period (start onward) structural break. returned break_mag median monitoring-period residual: negative means index dropped (e.g. vegetation loss / channel scour), positive means rose (establishment). break_date decimal year (e.g. 2022.42) NA break detected. Pixels reduced parallel parallel::mclapply() (forked workers, per-pixel logic parameters inherited directly). Pixels fewer min_obs valid observations short-circuit NA.

title: Per-pixel index trend over time — dft_rast_trend
text: two-band terra::SpatRaster layers trend (index change per year — negative = declining greenness, positive = recovering) trend_p (Mann-Kendall two-sided p-value; small = significant monotonic trend).

title: Per-pixel index trend over time — dft_rast_trend
text: slope Theil-Sen estimate — median pairwise slopes — resistant single anomalous season (e.g. smoke/drought year), unlike ordinary least-squares fit. Significance non-parametric Mann-Kendall test monotonic trend.

title: Fetch a masked spectral-index cube from a STAC catalog — dft_stac_cube
text: Sibling dft_stac_fetch() continuous change detection. dft_stac_fetch() materializes one categorical raster per year, builds sub-annual reflectance cube, masks clouds, computes spectral index band roles, returns index time series SpatRaster (one layer per time step) — input dft_rast_break() per-pixel trajectory breakpoint detection.

title: Fetch a masked spectral-index cube from a STAC catalog — dft_stac_cube
code: dft_stac_cube(   aoi,   source = "sentinel-2-l2a",   index = "kndvi",   datetime = NULL,   res = 10,   crs = NULL,   dt = "P1M",   aggregation = "median",   resampling = "bilinear",   clip = TRUE,   cloud_cover_max = 60,   months = NULL,   mask_values = NULL,   tile_size = NULL,   cache_dir = NULL,   force = FALSE,   sign_fn = rstac::sign_planetary_computer() )

title: Fetch a masked spectral-index cube from a STAC catalog — dft_stac_cube
text: aoi sf polygon defining area interest. source Character. cube source name dft_stac_config() (default "sentinel-2-l2a"). Must source cube = TRUE. index Character. Spectral index dft_index_table() (default "kndvi"). Determines band roles (thus assets) fetched. datetime Character. ISO 8601 interval "start/end". NULL, uses available_datetime dft_stac_config(). res Numeric. Output pixel size CRS units (default 10). crs Character. Target CRS EPSG string. NULL, auto-detected AOI centroid's UTM zone. dt Character. ISO 8601 duration temporal aggregation window (default "P1M", monthly). cadence dft_rast_break()'s frequency must agree . aggregation Character. Temporal aggregation multiple scenes one dt window (default "median"). resampling Character. Spatial resampling (default "bilinear"). clip Logical. TRUE (default), clip returned stack AOI polygon terra::mask() (cells outside → NA every layer), dft_rast_break() / dft_rast_trend() reduce -polygon pixels. Set FALSE keep wider extent (e.g. surrounding context, mask later different polygon). clips output — default tile_size = NULL full bbox COGs still streamed either way, clip = FALSE returns full bounding box. tile_size set read tiled, clip = FALSE returns AOI-intersecting tile union (stair-stepped superset polygon NA empty tiles skipped), gap-free bounding box. cloud_cover_max Numeric. Scene-level eo:cloud_cover maximum percent STAC pre-filter (default 60). months Integer vector calendar months (1-12) keep, NULL (default) . Restricting growing season (e.g. 6:9) sharpens vegetation signal — snow low-sun winter scenes carry vegetation information — cuts number scenes streamed. Months retained scenes become NA monthly cube, per-pixel series stays regular frequency = 12 dft_rast_break(). Prefer longer datetime window using , enough growing-season history remains fit stable BFAST baseline. mask_values Integer vector mask-band classes exclude. NULL, uses mask_values dft_stac_config() (e.g. Sentinel-2 SCL cloud / shadow / cirrus classes). tile_size Numeric NULL (default). Edge length, CRS units (metres default UTM CRS), read-tiling grid (#38). NULL, one cube streamed whole AOI bounding box (read scales bbox, AOI). set, bbox split grid tile_size-square tiles tiles intersect AOI polygon streamed, mosaicked — thin, diagonal AOI (e.g. floodplain corridor) reads close footprint. Snapped multiple res. Smaller tiles waste less bbox cost per-tile round trips; auto-tuning. cube always caches .tif either way; tiled read keys distinctly (see caching note ), untiled caches untouched tile_size = NULL byte--byte previous behavior. continuous-path twin dft_stac_fetch()'s tile_size — filter_geom-independent way bound read. cube resamples bilinear, tiled cube faithfully reproduces untiled cube (per-pixel reducers unaffected) lands bbox-anchored grid sub-pixel-offset — pixel-identical — untiled cube. cache_dir Character. Cache directory. NULL, uses dft_cache_path(). force Logical. Re-fetch even cached, overwriting cached raster (default FALSE). sign_fn signing function STAC assets. Default rstac::sign_planetary_computer().

title: Fetch a masked spectral-index cube from a STAC catalog — dft_stac_cube
text: terra::SpatRaster index stack — one layer per time step, time value per layer — cached GeoTIFF. default (clip = TRUE) stack clipped AOI polygon (cloud-masked, cells outside polygon NA), reduced raster dft_rast_break() already polygon-tight; pass clip = FALSE full AOI bounding box (, tile_size set, AOI-intersecting tile union). sources reflectance-offset baseline boundary (Sentinel-2), items split boundary offset-corrected per side, series crossing carries artificial index step.

title: Fetch a masked spectral-index cube from a STAC catalog — dft_stac_cube
text: index stack materialized GeoTIFF dft_cache_path() <source>/cube_<key>.tif, keyed hash AOI geometry every cube-affecting parameter (including clip tile_size, tiled read keys apart untiled one). invariant dft_rast_break()'s parameters, caching makes bfast parameter sweeps cheap — re-read local raster instead re-streaming COGs. Three STAC-query specifics distinguish cube mode dft_stac_fetch(): pagination via rstac::items_fetch() mandatory (monthly multi-year query returns hundreds items; single page silently truncates); query uses intersects AOI geometry, bounding box (floodplain polygons highly non-rectangular); scene-level eo:cloud_cover pre-filter shrinks collection pixel read, complementing per-pixel mask filtering.

title: Attribute transition patches from an overlay polygon layer — dft_transition_attribute
text: Tag change patches (dft_transition_vectors()) columns overlay polygon layer — fire perimeters, cutblocks, roads, tenures — help separate mapped transitions cause. Generic design: drift carries domain knowledge; caller supplies overlay, columns carry, (optionally) temporal filter.

title: Attribute transition patches from an overlay polygon layer — dft_transition_attribute
code: dft_transition_attribute(   patches,   overlay,   cols,   predicate = sf::st_intersects,   match_mode = c("all", "largest"),   time_col = NULL,   time_interval = NULL )

title: Attribute transition patches from an overlay polygon layer — dft_transition_attribute
text: patches sf object change patches, typically dft_transition_vectors(). overlay sf polygon layer attribute (e.g. fire perimeters, consolidated cutblocks). cols Character vector overlay column names carry onto patch. Must collide existing patches column names. predicate Spatial predicate function used match patches overlay features, e.g. sf::st_intersects() (default) sf::st_within(). applies match_mode = "": largest-overlap assignment inherently intersection-based (sf::st_join(largest = TRUE) ignores join predicate), combining custom predicate match_mode = "largest" error. match_mode patch assigned matches one overlay feature: "" (default) — plain left join; patch straddling k overlay features appears k times (patch_id repeats). "largest" — exactly one row per patch, assigned overlay feature greatest intersection area (via sf::st_join(largest = TRUE); matching intersection, see predicate). time_col Character NULL. Name numeric time column overlay used temporal filtering (e.g. FIRE_YEAR, HARVEST_YEAR). Must supplied together time_interval, must numeric scale (see Details). NULL (default) skips filter. time_interval Length-2 numeric NULL. transition interval scale time_col, e.g. c(2017, 2023) calendar years. Overlay features whose time_col falls outside interval (bounds inclusive) dropped joining; features NA time also dropped. Patches dft_transition_vectors() carry time columns, interval always supplied explicitly.

title: Attribute transition patches from an overlay polygon layer — dft_transition_attribute
text: patches cols columns joined (NA patch matches overlay feature). match_mode = "" patch matching several overlay features duplicated, one row per match; "largest" result exactly one row per input patch.

title: Attribute transition patches from an overlay polygon layer — dft_transition_attribute
text: overlay transformed CRS patches joining, run sf::st_make_valid() first — real-world disturbance perimeters routinely fail GEOS validity checks, otherwise break largest-overlap computation.

what: Temporal filter — how time_col and time_interval must be presented
title: Attribute transition patches from an overlay polygon layer — dft_transition_attribute
text: filter plain numeric comparison (overlay[[time_col]] >= time_interval[1] & <= time_interval[2]), scale-agnostic — numbers may calendar years, decimal years, months, epoch offsets — arguments must numeric scale. time_col must name numeric (integer double) column; passing Date POSIXct column hard error, silent mis-comparison. Values coerced rounded: decimal year like 2018.5 compared -. filter dates, convert numeric axis first, column interval: Calendar year (simplest annual disturbance data):   Epoch days (Date stores days since 1970-01-01, .numeric() coercion):   (POSIXct coerces seconds since epoch — keep interval seconds match.) Mixing scales (e.g. epoch-day column c(2017, 2023) year interval) error — silently matches nothing. Keep one axis.
code: overlay$yr <- as.numeric(format(overlay$burn_date, "%Y")) dft_transition_attribute(..., time_col = "yr", time_interval = c(2017, 2023)) overlay$t <- as.numeric(overlay$burn_date)               # days since epoch dft_transition_attribute(..., time_col = "t",   time_interval = as.numeric(as.Date(c("2017-01-01", "2023-12-31"))))

title: Attribute transition patches from an overlay polygon layer — dft_transition_attribute
text: overlay treated polygon layer. Passing point line geometries supported: match_mode = "largest" compares intersection area, zero non-polygon overlays. Use point--polygon join directly cases. Temporal filtering numeric-; Date/POSIXct columns must coerced caller (see Details).

what: drift: Detecting Riparian and Inland Floodplain Transitions — drift-package
title: drift: Detecting Riparian and Inland Floodplain Transitions — drift-package
text: Fetch STAC-hosted land cover rasters, classify summarize change time within user-provided AOI polygons, produce standardized maps, plots, tables reporting. Generic STAC pipeline works classified raster (Esri IO LULC, ESA WorldCover, custom COGs).

title: drift: Detecting Riparian and Inland Floodplain Transitions — drift-package
text: Maintainer: Allan Irvine al@newgraphenvironment.com (ORCID) contributors: New Graph Environment [copyright holder]

title: drift 0.8.0
text: dft_transition_attribute() tags change patches dft_transition_vectors() columns overlay polygon layer — fire perimeters, cutblocks, roads, tenures — driver can separate mapped transitions cause without hand-rolling spatial joins (#42). Deliberately generic: drift carries BC/domain knowledge; caller supplies overlay, columns carry (cols), optionally numeric temporal filter (time_col + time_interval, bounds inclusive) keeps overlay features whose time falls within transition interval — 2022 fire attributes 2017→2023 loss, 2012 fire . Two assignment modes patch straddles multiple overlay features: match_mode = "" (left join, one row per match) "largest" (one row per patch greatest overlap area). Largest-overlap assignment intersection-based, combining custom predicate error rather silent mis-attribution; overlay reprojected patch CRS run st_make_valid() automatically, since real-world disturbance perimeters routinely fail validity checks.

title: drift 0.7.0
text: dft_stac_cube() gains tile_size (default NULL), continuous-path twin dft_stac_fetch()’s tile_size (#36): opt-bounds STAC read AOI footprint (#38). default one gdalcubes cube streamed whole AOI bounding box, COG streaming — dominant cost (~10-30 min multi-year monthly Sentinel-2 fetch) — scales bbox, AOI; thin, diagonal floodplain corridor bbox largely empty. tile_size (CRS units — metres default UTM CRS) set, bbox split res-aligned grid tiles intersect AOI polygon streamed — carrying full SCL mask, spectral index, 2022 baseline-offset split — mosaicked terra::merge(), corridor reads close footprint. filter_geom-independent path (polygon clip -cube segfaults pinned gdalcubes build). cube always caches .tif tiled read keys distinctly, untiled caches untouched tile_size = NULL byte--byte previous behavior. cube resamples bilinear, tiled cube faithfully reproduces untiled cube (bilinear-aligned correlation ~0.997, per-layer means within ~1e-3, tile seams) lands bbox-anchored grid sub-pixel-offset — pixel-identical — untiled cube; offset immaterial per-pixel dft_rast_break() / dft_rast_trend() reducers. tile_size set, clip = FALSE returns AOI-intersecting tile union (NA empty tiles skipped), gap-free bounding box.

title: drift 0.6.0
text: dft_stac_fetch() gains tile_size (default NULL), opt-bounds STAC download AOI footprint (#36). default single cube streamed whole AOI bounding box, thin, diagonal floodplain corridor (measured ~10% bbox inside polygon) roughly 10× pixels downloaded AOI needs. tile_size (CRS units — metres default UTM CRS) set, bbox split res-aligned grid tiles intersect AOI polygon streamed, mosaicked terra::merge() — corridor fetches close footprint. Smaller tiles waste less bbox cost per-tile round trips (auto-tuning). filter_geom-independent path (polygon-clip cube pipeline segfaults pinned gdalcubes build). Output byte-identical previous version, verified golden snapshot across full parameter matrix. dft_transition_vectors() gains changes_only (default FALSE): TRUE, stable (== ) transitions dropped raster level polygonizing, terra::.polygons() builds geometry actual change patches. fragmented floodplain — stable mosaic grid polygonization dominates memory — roughly halves peak use (9M-cell, 415k-patch benchmark went 3.83 GB 1.71 GB). result equals default output filtered change patches. patch_area_min set, small patches also dropped polygonizing, identical output. patch_id dft_transition_vectors() numbered surviving patches filtering drops , empty result now carries zone column per-zone results bind cleanly.

title: drift 0.3.0
text: Continuous index-trajectory change detection floodplain reaches (#30). new fetch--reduce pipeline complements categorical dft_stac_fetch() path. dft_stac_cube() builds cloud-masked monthly spectral-index stack Sentinel-2 (via new "sentinel-2-l2a" source); dft_rast_break() reduces per pixel bfast::bfastmonitor() two-band raster abrupt break date magnitude; dft_rast_trend() reduces per-pixel gradual trend — robust Theil-Sen slope (index change per year) Mann-Kendall significance — degradation/recovery monitoring annual labels show. Together let continuous trajectory validate categorical land-cover transitions (confirming mapped losses carry real spectral decline) detect gradual change. See “Trajectories Check Land-Cover Change” vignette. dft_index_expr() dft_index_table() add table-driven spectral-index registry (NDVI, kNDVI, NDMI) whose formulas written band roles, one index resolves reflectance source; reflectance scale/offset folded expression. Sentinel-2 handling correctness-focused: dft_stac_cube() masks cloud/shadow/cirrus/snow, restricts caller-chosen calendar months (e.g. growing season) sharpen signal cut scenes streamed, — +1000 DN reflectance offset applies processing baseline 04.00 (2022-01-25) — splits items boundary corrects side, multi-year series carries artificial index step 2022. dft_stac_config() gains role-based schema reflectance cube sources (band roles, mask classes, scale/offset, offset boundary), leaving categorical io-lulc/esa-worldcover sources unchanged. bfast added Suggests. Known limitation tracked follow-: cube spans AOI bounding box rather polygon (gdalcubes filter_geom limitation, #32); labelling breaks /land-cover classes #31.

title: drift 0.2.4
text: dft_transition_vectors() longer exhausts memory large-extent rasters (#27). per-class loop allocated full-grid vectors per class per patch — ncell × n_patches churn OOM-killed 102.6M-cell, 56-class floodplain. Replaced single terra::patches(values = TRUE) pass plus sparse patch--label map. Output identical (verified patch--patch old implementation); patch_id numbering / row order changes, raster scan order. Benchmark 24M cells: 1.9 s 4,799-patch raster; old code took 122 s milder 1,232-patch raster size. terra dependency floored >= 1.8-10: earlier versions edge-wraparound bug patches(values = TRUE) silently merged patches touching opposite raster edges.

title: drift 0.2.3
text: Fix silent cross-AOI cache collision dft_stac_fetch() (#25). Cache files keyed source + year , fetching second AOI source/year silently returned first AOI’s raster masked second AOI’s extent. Cache filenames now include hash AOI geometry fetch-affecting parameters (res, crs, dt, aggregation, resampling, stac_url, collection, asset). Existing caches re-fetch first use upgrading; dft_cache_clear() reclaims orphaned old-format files. force = TRUE now overwrites cached file instead erroring “File already exists” (#25).
